user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see which AI models are available on my plan so that I can make an informed model selection.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/settings/available-models/ requires IsAuthenticated. 404 if no tenant. Returns {models: [{id, label, input_per_million, output_per_million}], preferred_model, model_tier, task_model_preferences, task_slugs}. Models are the union of tier base + live free offer + BYO extras, sorted alphabetically by label. Rates from billing.constants.MODEL_RATES.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to assign different AI models to different scheduled tasks so that I can optimize cost and quality per task type.
expected_behaviour: PATCH /api/v1/tenants/settings/task-model-preferences/ requires IsAuthenticated. Body: {task_model_preferences: {slug: model_id, ...}}. Valid slugs: heartbeat, morning_briefing, evening_checkin, week_review, background_tasks (400 for others). model_id validated against allowed models (400 if not allowed). Empty string clears a slug. Merges into existing preferences. Saves, bumps pending_config, enqueues immediate apply. Returns {task_model_preferences}.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to link my Telegram account to my NBHD United user so that I can chat with my assistant through Telegram.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/tenants/telegram/generate-link/ requires IsAuthenticated. Returns 400 if already linked (user.telegram_user_id set). Generates a 32-byte urlsafe token, creates TelegramLinkToken with expires_at=now+15min. Returns {deep_link: https://t.me/<BOT>?start=<token>, qr_code: data:image/png;base64,..., expires_at}. Linking completes when user sends /start <token> to the Telegram bot — handled by telegram_service.process_start_token(), which: validates token not expired/used, links telegram_user_id/chat_id/username to User, marks token used, triggers update_tenant_config via QStash if tenant is ACTIVE.
dependencies: apps/router (Telegram bot webhook processes /start), QStash (update_tenant_config), qrcode library
inventory_notes: Tokens expire in 15 minutes. process_start_token is called from the router/Telegram bot webhook, not from this API. A cron task (cleanup_expired_telegram_tokens) purges tokens older than 1 hour.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to unlink my Telegram account so that I can reconnect it or remove the channel.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/tenants/telegram/unlink/ requires IsAuthenticated. Returns 400 if not linked. Clears telegram_user_id, telegram_chat_id, telegram_username on User. If preferred_channel was 'telegram' and LINE is linked, switches preferred_channel to 'line'. Calls invalidate_cache(old_chat_id) from apps/router. Returns {success: True}.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to check if my Telegram account is linked so that I can see the current connection state.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/telegram/status/ requires IsAuthenticated. Returns {linked: true, telegram_username, telegram_chat_id} if linked, {linked: false} otherwise.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to link my LINE account so that I can chat with my assistant through LINE.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/tenants/line/generate-link/ requires IsAuthenticated. Returns 400 if already linked (user.line_user_id set). Delegates to apps/router/line_service.generate_link_token(). Returns {deep_link, qr_code, expires_at}. Linking completes via LINE webhook (separate flow in apps/router/line_urls).

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to unlink my LINE account so that I can remove the channel.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/tenants/line/unlink/ requires IsAuthenticated. Returns 400 if not linked. Delegates to apps/router/line_service.unlink_line(). Returns {success: True}.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my LINE link status and whether LINE messaging is at capacity so that I can decide which channel to use.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/line/status/ requires IsAuthenticated. Returns apps/router/line_service.get_line_status(user) merged with {quota: {exhausted, checked_at, exhausted_at}} from LineQuotaState.get(). The quota exhausted flag lets the frontend disable the LINE channel selector.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to set my preferred messaging channel (Telegram or LINE) so that proactive messages go to the right app.
expected_behaviour: PATCH /api/v1/tenants/line/preferred-channel/ requires IsAuthenticated. Accepts {preferred_channel: 'telegram'|'line'}. 400 for invalid value. 400 if LINE selected but user.line_user_id not set. 400 if Telegram selected but user.telegram_chat_id not set. If LINE selected: checks is_quota_exhausted() — if exhausted, returns 409 with code=line_quota_exhausted. Saves user.preferred_channel. Returns {preferred_channel, message}.
inventory_notes: Server-side quota gate enforced even if the frontend UI already disables the LINE option.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see all the PII placeholders detected in my chats so that I can review and correct the name bindings.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/settings/entity-registry/ requires IsAuthenticated. Iterates tenant.pii_entity_map via iter_normalized() (coerces legacy string-shaped entries to dict). Returns {entries: [{placeholder, name, relationship, notes, updated_at}]} sorted by placeholder. 404 if no tenant.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to edit the name, relationship, or notes for a PII placeholder so that rehydration uses the correct context.
expected_behaviour: PATCH /api/v1/tenants/settings/entity-registry/<placeholder>/ requires IsAuthenticated. 404 if placeholder not in pii_entity_map. Accepts {name (max 200 chars), relationship (max 80 chars), notes (max 500 chars)}; any subset. Validates types and lengths (400). Coerces current entry, applies patch fields, stamps updated_at. Saves via Tenant.objects.filter(pk=).update() (no full save). Returns {placeholder, name, relationship, notes, updated_at}.
inventory_notes: Uses filter().update() to avoid racing with concurrent saves on other fields.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to remove a PII placeholder entry so that it no longer appears in my privacy settings.
expected_behaviour: DELETE /api/v1/tenants/settings/entity-registry/<placeholder>/ requires IsAuthenticated. 404 if placeholder not in pii_entity_map. Removes the key from entity_map dict. Updates via Tenant.objects.filter(pk=).update(). Returns 204.
inventory_notes: Deletion does NOT update the denylist — rehydration of existing workspace file references still works via the entity_map entry being gone (they will no longer be rehydrated).

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see words I've marked as non-PII so that I can review my redaction exclusions.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/settings/pii-denylist/ requires IsAuthenticated. Returns {entries: [{key, reason, decided_at}]} sorted by key. Entries come from tenant.pii_denylist JSONField. 404 if no tenant.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to mark a word as non-PII so that it is never redacted in my messages.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/tenants/settings/pii-denylist/ requires IsAuthenticated. Body: {name: string}. 400 if not a string, empty, or >200 chars. Calls normalize_denylist_key(name) — 400 if canonicalizes to empty. Adds key to tenant.pii_denylist with {reason: 'manual', decided_at: now}. Saves via filter().update(). Returns {key, reason, decided_at} with 201.
inventory_notes: Adding to denylist does NOT remove the entity_map entry — rehydration of existing placeholders still works; only new redaction is suppressed.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to bulk-add multiple names to the PII denylist in one request so that the operation completes quickly.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/tenants/settings/pii-denylist/bulk/ requires IsAuthenticated. Body: {names: [string]}. 400 if names is not a list. 400 if batch >1000. Iterates entries: skips non-strings, empty (after strip), >200 chars, or entries that canonicalize to empty — each skipped entry recorded with reason. Valid entries added to denylist in one filter().update(). Returns {added: [keys], skipped: [{name, reason}]} with 200.
inventory_notes: URL order matters: 'bulk/' must be registered before '<str:key>/' in urls.py to prevent 'bulk' being captured as a key for PIIDenylistItemView.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to remove a word from my PII denylist so that the redactor can protect it again.
expected_behaviour: DELETE /api/v1/tenants/settings/pii-denylist/<key>/ requires IsAuthenticated. 404 if key not in denylist. Removes entry, saves via filter().update(). Returns 204.
inventory_notes: Removal re-enables redaction for that canonical key on future messages.

user_story: As a user receiving a promotional email, I want to redeem a trial extension link so that I get additional free access time.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/promos/redeem/?code=<campaign>&token=<hmac> is unauthenticated (AllowAny). Validates code and token params (redirects to /promo/redeemed?status=invalid if missing). Looks up PromoCampaign by code (invalid if not found). Checks valid_until (expired if past). Verifies HMAC token via promo_signing.verify_promo_token() (invalid if bad). Looks up User by decoded user_id (invalid if not found). If user has no tenant: records outcome=no_tenant, redirects invalid. If tenant has active Stripe subscription: records outcome=already_subscribed, redirects active_subscription. Otherwise: extends trial (base=max(now, existing trial_ends_at) + extension_days), inserts PromoRedemption row (unique_together constraint handles double-click idempotency — redirects already on IntegrityError), updates Tenant.trial_ends_at + is_trial=True + status=ACTIVE. Redirects to /promo/redeemed?status=success.
inventory_notes: PromoRedemption insert is done OUTSIDE the tenant update transaction to use the unique_together constraint as the race guard. Tenant update failure rolls back the redemption row so retries can re-attempt.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to create a long-lived API token so that external apps like YardTalk can authenticate to my account.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/auth/tokens/create/ requires IsAuthenticated. Throttled: 10 PATs per hour per user (UserPATMintHourThrottle). Body: {name (max 255, required), scopes (optional list, default ['sessions:write','sessions:read']), expires_in_days (optional, 1-365)}. Validates scopes against ALLOWED_PAT_SCOPES {'sessions:write','sessions:read'} (400 for unknown). Generates 32-byte urlsafe secret prefixed 'pat_'; stores SHA-256 hash and 8-char prefix. Returns {id, name, token (raw, shown once), token_prefix, scopes, expires_at, created_at, warning} with 201.
inventory_notes: Raw token shown exactly once and not stored. Caller must save it securely.
